revision.text:
[[ja:頗梨采女]][[zh:頗梨采女]] {{Infobox deity}} [[File:Harisainyo.png|thumb|130px|頗梨采天女
仏像図彙 (1783rd year )]] '''Harisai-sho''' (頗梨采女, はりさいじょ、はりさいにょ) is a deity regarded as the consort of {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}. It is also written as '''Hari-sai-jo''' and '''Hari-sai-nyo''' (波利采女、波利賽女). One theory holds that the origin of her name is derived from the Sanskrit word {{ill|Sanskrit|ja|梵語|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanskrit|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サンスクリット|1=Sanskrit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11059}} “Hari” (meaning "crystal"). == 神仏習合と分離 == {{!}}Syncretism and Separation of Shinto and Buddhism{{!}} {{indent}} {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|qid=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}, a deity of Buddhist origin revered as the guardian deity of {{ill|Jetavana|en|Jetavana|qid=Q305242|lt=Jetavana monastery|1=Jetavana}}, was feared in Japan as a god of epidemics (one who brings on plagues) and was syncretized with the Shinto deity {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. Until the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri Rei|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri Rei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=Shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1970535}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明治|1=Meiji era|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3125096}}, he was enshrined as a festival deity at Gion Shrine ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|qid=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine|1=Yasaka Shrine}}) and received profound veneration. Because Harisai-sho is considered the consort of Gyūto Tennen, she was also identified with {{ill|Kiinada-hime|ja|奇稲田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiinada-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クシナダヒメ|1=Kushinadahime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q505469}}, the consort of Susanoo-no-Mikoto. {{endindent}} Originally, Harisai-sho was enshrined in the west side of the main hall at Gion-sha, but after the Meiji period at Yasaka Shrine she has been enshrined as Kiinada-hime in the east chamber. == 伝承 == {{!}}Tradition{{!}} {{indent}} She is said to be one of the pillars of the {{ill|Eight Great Dragon Kings|ja|八大竜王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Great Dragon Kings|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Sagararyūō|ja|娑伽羅龍王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagararyūō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and is also regarded as the mother of the {{ill|Hasshōjin|ja|八将神|1=Eight Directional Deities|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshōjin|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Directional Deities|qq=}}. According to the ''{{ill|Iroha Jiruishō|en|Iroha Jiruishō|qid=Q11614525|lt=Shikihabiruiryūshō|1=Iroha Jiruishō}}'' in Gion, it is stated that, as the consort of Gyūto Tennen—referred to there as {{ill|Sagararyūjo|ja|娑伽羅竜女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagararyūjo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—she gave birth to the Hasshōjin and 84,654 gods{{Cite web|和書 |coauthors=橘忠兼 |url=https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/ho02/ho02_00596/ho02_00596_0003/ho02_00596_0003_p0046.jpg |title=祇園 |work=色葉字類抄　享保8th year 日野資時の写本 |publisher=早稲田大学　古典籍総合データベース |accessdate=2011-02-12 }}. In the ''{{ill|Hōgai Naiden|ja|簠簋内伝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōgai Naiden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三国相伝陰陽輨轄簠簋内伝金烏玉兎集|1=Shinnaiden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11277704}}'', she is described as “Toshitoku Harisai-sho, mother of the Hasshōjin, possessing a beautiful countenance, forbearance, and a compassionate form,” thereby being equated with the directional deity {{ill|Toshitoku Shin|ja|歳徳神|1=Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku Shin|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|qq=}} (although there are dissenting opinions). In the ''{{ill|Gion Gyūto Tennen Engi|ja|祇園牛頭天王縁起|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Gyūto Tennen Engi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'', it is stated that she dwells in the Sagararyū Palace of the Southern Sea, has two elder sisters—{{ill|Kanbira Onna|ja|金毘羅女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanbira Onna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Fumyō Onna|ja|婦命女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fumyō Onna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—and is regarded as the third daughter of the Dragon King[https://blog.goo.ne.jp/gozutennou/e/bcec8c2a67b19fbe94ac06cbdff681c7 京都大学付属図書館蔵『牛頭天王御縁起』より]. {{endindent}} == 脚注・References == = == See Also === * {{ill|Somin Shōrai|en|Somin Shōrai|qid=Q7560421|lt=Somin Shōrai|1=Somin Shōrai}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|qid=Q11588425|lt=Gion Belief|1=Gion Faith}} * {{ill|Tawashima God|ja|淡島神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tawashima God|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1575740}} * {{ill|Hari-Kuyō|en|Hari-Kuyō|qid=Q1585326|lt=Needle Memorial Service|1=Hari-Kuyō}} * {{ill|Zennyo Ryūō|en|Zennyo Ryūō|qid=Q5931305|lt=Zenjo Ryūō|1=Zennyo Ryūō}} (traditionally regarded as the third daughter of Sagararyūō, like Harisai-sho) {{Buddhism-stub}} [[ja:頗梨采女]] [[ja:頗梨采天女]] [[zh:頗梨采女]] {{translated page|ja|頗梨采女|version=101010578|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q1654753}} [[Category:Deva (Buddhism)]] [[Category:Gion cult]] [[Category:Onmyōdō deities]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu_shūgō]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
